info.name: Starter Kit API
info.description: Full Postman collection for the starter-kit service. Login first to populate access_token and refresh_token collection variables. Auth responses use access_token; there is no legacy data.token field.

item.name: Auth

request.description: Use this first to check whether public self-registration is enabled. When disabled, Register, Send Register OTP, and first-time Google auto-registration should be rejected.
Use this when auth.public_registration_enabled=true and auth.register_otp_enabled=false. If public registration is disabled, this request should return 403.
Only use this when auth.public_registration_enabled=true and auth.register_otp_enabled=true. If public registration is disabled, this request should return 403.
Use this when auth.public_registration_enabled=true and auth.register_otp_enabled=true. First call Send Register OTP, then replace otp_code with the code received by email.
identifier accepts email or phone.
Alternative login flow. identifier accepts phone and will be normalized by the backend.
Existing Google-linked users can still log in. If auth.public_registration_enabled=false, first-time Google login must not auto-create a new user.

Controlled by auth.password_reset_email_enabled. If false, response data can contain a development reset token and this script stores it as reset_token. If true, the reset token is sent by email in the frontend reset link query param.
Use the same token from the email reset link query param, for example /reset-password?token=<token>, as the JSON body token.

item.description: Public registration is controlled by app_configs key auth.public_registration_enabled. If false, register-related endpoints and first-time Google login registration are blocked. Registration OTP is controlled separately by auth.register_otp_enabled. Password reset email delivery is controlled by auth.password_reset_email_enabled.

request.description: Destructive. Usually use only on a disposable account.
Destructive. Use with care.
Destructive. Use with care.
Destructive. Use with care.

item.name: Configurations

request.description: Use this before testing public registration, OTP, or password reset flows. Copy the id for auth.public_registration_enabled, auth.register_otp_enabled, or auth.password_reset_email_enabled into {{config_id}}, then call Update Configuration.

Update an existing config by id. For auth.public_registration_enabled, auth.register_otp_enabled, or auth.password_reset_email_enabled, set value to "true" to enable the flow or "false" to disable it, with is_active=true.

item.description: Runtime configs can be changed without code changes. To test auth flows, list auth configs, copy the config id into {{config_id}}, then update value to "true" or "false".

request.description: Registered only when Redis is available.

item.name: Locations

request.description: Read provinces from Redis cache first, then PostgreSQL.
Read cities by province_code from Redis cache first, then PostgreSQL.
Read districts by city_code from Redis cache first, then PostgreSQL.
Read villages by district_code from Redis cache first, then PostgreSQL.
Start an asynchronous province-only sync job. Payload requires only `year` and `level=province`. The returned job ID is automatically saved into `location_sync_job_id`.
Start an asynchronous city sync job for one province. Required payload: `level=city` and `province_code`.
Start an asynchronous district sync job for one city. Required payload: `level=district`, `province_code`, and `city_code`.
Start an asynchronous village sync job for one district. Required payload: `level=village`, `province_code`, `city_code`, and `district_code`.
Start a full asynchronous hierarchical import. Use this for initial bootstrap only because it is much heavier than scoped sync requests.

Read the current status of an asynchronous location sync job. `location_sync_job_id` is filled automatically after running one of the sync requests above. Status values: `queued`, `running`, `completed`, `failed`.

item.description: Runtime reads use Redis then PostgreSQL. External API is used only by the sync endpoint to refresh database data.
